title: Assistant Nurse Clinician
description:
Responsibilities:

Clinical Activities

The Assistant Nurse Clinician ensures high standards of clinical practiceand continuity of care by:

·  Providing direct patient care and acting as a clinical role model for best nursing practices

·  Supporting complex clinical decision-making and timely escalation of patient care concerns

·  Participating in ward rounds to monitor patient condition, care quality, and safety

·  Ensuring adherence to nursing protocols, care plans, and clinical SOPs

·  Supporting care of high-acuity or high-risk patients (e.g. falls risk, pressure injuries, Multi-Drug Resistant Organisms (MDROs), tracheostomy care, wound management)

·  Ensuring effective clinical handovers and continuity of care across shifts

·  Providing clinical support during emergencies and deteriorating patient situations

·  Support in Code Blue training sessions for the staff

·  Participating actively in clinical improvement and practice enhancement projects

·  Disseminate and implement clinical operational policies relating to direct client care procedures.

·  Assist in conducting clinical competency and providing orientation to new staff onboard.

Staff Supervision & Development

The Assistant Nurse Clinician supports staff capability development by:

·  Guiding and supporting junior nurses and healthcare assistants in daily clinical work

·  Providing bedside coaching, mentoring, and constructive feedback

·  Supervise daily nursing care routines and procedures as a resource personnel to ensure quality of care provision.

·  Supporting orientation and on-the-job training for new hires and locum staff

·  Supporting the trained Preceptors in providing the relevant coaching and mentorship to new hires.

·  Assisting with clinical competency assessments and skills validation

·  Reinforcing professional conduct, accountability, and ethical nursing practice

·  Supporting ANM/NM in identifying training needs and education gaps

·  Promoting a culture of learning, teamwork, and professionalism

·  Conduct regular clinical trainings and workshops for clinical team.

·  Participate actively in clinical improvement projects

Quality, Patient Safety & Clinical Governance

The Assistant Nurse Clinician contributes to quality and patient safetyinitiatives by:

·  Participating in quality improvement activities aligned with unit and organisational goals

·  Supporting clinical audits (e.g. medication safety, falls prevention, infection control, documentation compliance, etc)

·  Identifying clinical practice gaps and supporting corrective actions

·  Promoting safe care practices and compliance with reporting requirements

·  Encouraging incident reporting and open communication related to patient safety

·  Supporting implementation of evidence-based practices at the point of care

Resource & Manpower Coordination

The Assistant Nurse Clinician supports efficient use of resources by:

·  Supporting shift workflow and appropriate task allocation within the team

·  Assisting ANM/NM in ensuring adequate skill mix during shifts

·  Supporting coverage during staff shortages or unexpected absences

·  Practising stewardship in the use of consumables and clinical equipment

·  Ensuring readiness and availability of essential ward equipment

Regulatory Compliance & Audits

The Assistant Nurse Clinician supports compliance and governance by:

·  Adhering to Hospital to Home (HHOM), Ministry of Health (MOH) licensing requirements, and Singapore Nursing Board (SNB) professional standards

·  Ensuring compliance with organisational policies, Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), and clinical guidelines

·  Supporting audit readiness through proper clinical documentation and practices

·  Support in internal and external audits and inspections

·  Maintaining high infection prevention and control standards

·  Highlighting non-compliance or practice gaps to nursing leadership promptly

·  Support in accreditation and licensing preparation exercises

·  Support in documentation readiness for audits and regulatory reporting

·  Support in data required to meet audit and performance management targets in a timely manner.

Administrative Reporting & Documentation

The Assistant Nurse Clinician ensures accurate and timelydocumentation by:

·  Maintaining clear, accurate, and timely nursing documentation in clinical systems

·  Supporting incident reporting, Root Cause Analyse (RCA) and follow-up documentation as required

·  Assisting in maintaining competency and training records when assigned

·  Communicating effectively with nursing leaders and multidisciplinary teams

Systems and Administrative Support

·  Support clinical workflow coordination

·  Respond to internal queries related to clinical and nursing matters

·  Provide administrative support for clinical quality improvement initiatives

·  Comply with organisational standards, procedures, protocols, and policies

·  Adhere strictly to the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA) relating to patients and staff

·  Perform other duties as assigned by the Reporting Officer / Nurse Manager

Requirements:

· Possess a Registered Nursing Certificate

· Registered with Singapore Nursing Board

· Diploma in Nursing

· Post-basic or Advanced Diploma in Medical, Geriatrics, Palliative Care or relevant specialty is preferred

· Possess a professional demeanour and conduct themselves with integrity and respect towards all patients, colleagues and other parties

· Demonstrate patience and approachability while assistingpatients and handling challenging situations effectively

·      Demonstrate effective communication skills, both verbally and in writing, to interact with patients, colleagues and other parties

· Display adaptability and flexibility to work efficiently in diverse settings with changing demands and schedules

· Familiarity with regulatory guidelines and compliance standards.

· Possess good interpersonal and communication skills, with a positive work attitude and strong commitment towards service excellence

· Be a mature and objective lead, possess leadership qualities and positive interpersonal skills.

· Demonstrate high professional standards, integrity and ethicalconduct

· Exhibit strong leadership presence and emotional intelligence

· Display adaptability and resilience in dynamic healthcare environments

· Possess up-to-date Basic Cardia Life Support (BCLS) + Automated External Defibrillator (AED) Certificate.

· Must be able to work shift, occasional weekends and night shifts and public holidays monthly.

· Willing to go on rotational on-call roster weekly.

Work Location- 1800 Upper Changi Road North Singapore 507695

Please submit your application via below link:

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSf1gwpmjTSjoXIkh2dbZmgR2Swm9UNAjOZ8lDidwR0jP0W3Gw/viewform?fbzx=8506684303558776261

postedCompany.description:
Your Health Comes First

Welcome to PanCare Medical Clinic. Our team is dedicated to providing patients with comprehensive services to benefit their health and well-being. With our streamlined registration system and responsive diagnosis procedure, we have more time to focus on helping patients find treatments to feel better, and connecting them with resources to help them stay that way.

Primary Care

Ranging from acute illnesses to chronic diseases, our team is dedicated to be your partner in providing well-rounded care for you and your family.

Health Screening

PanCare provides a wide range of health screening services personalized for each individual.

Vaccinations and Travel Medicine

We get you ready for your travel plans, caring for you so that you can enjoy yourself with a peace of mind.

Eye Screening

Eye screening services are available for all patients from young to old.

Skin and Hair

Eczema, acne, hair loss or other skin problems? PanCare will be able to help.


Minor Surgery

PanCare can also help you with your ingrown toe nails, viral warts, stitches for wounds or other minor surgeries.
postedCompany.name: PANCARE MEDICAL CLINIC PTE. LTD.
